classes.label: ProfileCache
description:
A stable, session-scoped cache for user profiles.

Replaces the previous `useState<Map>`-based `UserProfileStore` with a plain
class that avoids React state identity changes on every cache update. Uses
`useSyncExternalStore` for reactivity: only subscribers re-render when
profiles are loaded.

Key properties:
- **Stable reference**: created once via `useRef`, never changes identity.
- **`requested` set**: tracks in-flight requests so concurrent callers don't
  issue duplicate `bulkResolve` calls. UIDs omitted from a response are
  cleared so future calls can retry.
- **Built-in batcher**: `loadOne` batches individual requests within a 50ms
  window via `createBatcher`.
- **No React dependencies**: can be extracted to core later.

children.label: resolve
description: Synchronous lookup by UID. Returns `undefined` if not cached.

children.label: getAll
description: Get all cached profiles.

children.label: ensureLoaded
description:
Bulk load with in-flight dedup via `requested` set.

UIDs already in the cache or previously requested are skipped. This makes
concurrent calls from priming, facets, and cells safe without external
dedup logic.

children.label: loadOne
description:
Single UID load — batched via `createBatcher` (50ms window).

Multiple cells mounting in the same frame get their `loadOne` calls
batched into one `bulkResolve`.

children.label: seed
description:
Inject already-resolved profiles into the cache without a network call.

Use when profiles have been fetched through an external path (e.g. a
custom `FilterFacetConfig.getFacets`) and should be available for
synchronous lookups via `resolve`/`getAll`. Does not overwrite existing
entries.

children.label: subscribe
description: Subscribe to cache changes. Returns an unsubscribe function.

children.label: getSnapshot
description: Returns the current version (increments on every merge).

functions.label: ContentListProvider
description:
Main provider component for content list functionality, including data fetching
(via React Query), sorting, search, and tags filtering.

Props like `dataSource`, `features`, and `services` should be stable references to avoid
unnecessary re-renders. Configuration from `features.sorting`, `features.pagination`, and
`features.search` is read once at mount; use a `key` prop to remount if you need to change
initial state dynamically.

Service-dependent features wrap children with the appropriate context provider:

- **Tags** (`services.tags`): wraps with `ContentManagementTagsProvider`, enabling tag
  display and filtering. The service's `parseSearchQuery` (if present) is passed through
  to support extracting tag filters from the search bar query text.
- **Favorites** (`services.favorites`): wraps with `FavoritesContextProvider`, enabling
  star buttons and starred filtering.
- **User profiles** (`services.userProfiles`): wraps with `ProfileCacheContext.Provider`,
  providing a shared
for synchronous profile resolution.
signature: ({ children, dataSource, labels, item, isReadOnly, id, queryKeyScope: queryKeyScopeProp, features, services, profileCache, }:

children.label:
{
  children,
  dataSource,
  labels,
  item,
  isReadOnly,
  id,
  queryKeyScope: queryKeyScopeProp,
  features = {},
  services,
  profileCache,
}

functions.label: DeleteConfirmationComponent
description:
Presentational confirmation modal for delete operations.

Stateless — all data and callbacks are provided via props.
Use

for the connected version that
reads from content list context.
signature: ({ items, entityName, entityNamePlural, isDeleting, error, onCancel, onConfirm, }:

children.label:
{
  items,
  entityName,
  entityNamePlural,
  isDeleting,
  error,
  onCancel,
  onConfirm,
}

functions.label: DeleteConfirmationModal
description:
Connected confirmation modal for delete operations.

Reads `item.onDelete` and entity labels from the provider config, calls
`refetch` on success, and manages `isDeleting`/`error` locally.
Delegates rendering to

functions.label: getCreatorKey
description:
Resolve the effective creator key for an item.

Maps `managed` items to

and items without a
`createdBy` to

. All other items return
their `createdBy` UID. Direct `ContentListProvider` consumers should use
this when implementing `findItems` filtering against `createdBy` values,
which may contain sentinel keys.
signature: (item: { managed?: boolean | undefined; createdBy?: string | undefined; }) => string

functions.label: getIncludeExcludeFlag
description: Returns the filter value as

if it has a `state` field;
otherwise `undefined`.

functions.label: isFilterFacetConfig
description: Type guard to check if a filter feature config is a
object (not boolean).

functions.label: isPaginationConfig
description: Type guard to check if pagination config is a
object (not boolean).

functions.label: isSearchConfig
description: Type guard to check if search config is a
object (not boolean).

functions.label: isSortingConfig
description: Type guard to check if sorting config is a
object (not boolean).

functions.label: toFindItemsFilters
description: Convert a
to the

shape
expected by `findItems`.

Maps:
- `model.search` → `filters.search`
- `model.flags[key]` → `filters[key]` as `IncludeExcludeFlag`
- `model.filters[field]` → `filters[field]` as `IncludeExcludeFilter`

functions.label: useContentListConfig
description:
Hook to access the content list configuration context.

This is a low-level hook that provides access to configuration and support flags.
For most use cases, prefer the feature-specific hooks like `useContentListItems`,
`useContentListSort`, etc.
returnComment: The content list context including configuration and support flags.

functions.label: useContentListFilters
description:
Hook to read the current filter state and clear all filters.

Derives `ActiveFilters` from `queryText` via `useActiveFilters`.

`clearFilters` strips structured filter/flag clauses from `queryText`
while preserving free-text search. For example, clearing
`"tag:production is:starred my search"` yields `"my search"`.

functions.label: useContentListItems
description: Hook to access the current list of items and loading state.
signature: <Record<string, unknown>>[]; totalItems: number; isLoading: boolean; isFetching: boolean; error: Error | undefined; refetch: () => Promise<void>; }
returnComment: Object containing items, totalItems, isLoading, isFetching, error, and refetch.

functions.label: useContentListPagination
description:
Hook to access and control pagination functionality.

Use this hook when you need to read or update the current page.
When pagination is disabled via `features.pagination: false`, `setPageIndex`
and `setPageSize` become no-ops and `isSupported` returns `false`.
returnComment: Object containing pagination state and control functions.

functions.label: useContentListSearch
description:
Hook to access and control the search query.

`queryText` is the source of truth — read directly from state.
The structured model (

) is derived on-demand
by consumers that need it (via `useQueryModel`).

functions.label: useContentListSelection
description:
Hook to access and control item selection.

Provides the current selection state and functions to modify it.
When selection is disabled (via `features.selection: false` or `isReadOnly`),
`setSelection` and `clearSelection` become no-ops and `isSupported` returns `false`.
returnComment: Object containing selection state, mutation functions, and support flag.

functions.label: useContentListSort
description:
Hook to access and control sorting functionality.

Use this hook when you need to read or update the sort configuration.
When sorting is disabled via `features.sorting: false`, `setSort` becomes a no-op
and `isSupported` returns `false`.
returnComment: Object containing field, direction, setSort function, and isSupported flag.

functions.label: useContentListState
description:
Hook to access the content list state and dispatch function.

This is a low-level hook. For most use cases, prefer the feature-specific hooks
like `useContentListSort`, `useContentListItems`, etc.
returnComment: The state context value including state, dispatch, and refetch.

functions.label: useCreatedByFilterToggle
description: Convenience wrapper around
for the `createdBy` field.

functions.label: useDeleteConfirmation
description: Encapsulates the open/close state for a

.

Consumers call `requestDelete(items)` to open the modal and render
`deleteModal` in their JSX. The modal handles confirmation, deletion
(via the provider's `onDelete`), loading, and error display internally.

functions.label: useFilterFacets
description:
React Query hook that calls a filter feature's `getFacets` callback.

Returns display-ready

arrays for filter popovers.
The `T` parameter carries through to the facet `data` field so renderers
receive typed payloads without casting.

Disabled when the feature is `true` (no config) or `false` — only fires
when a

with `getFacets` is provided.

Call with `enabled: isPopoverOpen` so it fires lazily on popover open.
signature: <T = unknown>(filterId: string, opts?: { enabled?: boolean | undefined; } | undefined) =>

children.label: filterId
description: - The filter dimension key (e.g. `'createdBy'`, `'tag'`).

children.label: opts
description: - Options including `enabled` to control when the query fires.

functions.label: useFilterToggle
description:
Generic hook for toggling a value in any field filter via EUI Query mutations.

Parses the current `queryText` → mutates the EUI Query → dispatches the
new text. Same pattern as `useFieldQueryFilter` in the toolbar, but works
from outside EuiSearchBar (e.g., avatar clicks, tag badge clicks).

For known filters, prefer the convenience wrappers
and

children.label: fieldName
description: - The field name (e.g., `'tag'`, `'createdBy'`).

functions.label: useProfile
description:
Resolve a single profile by UID. Self-loading — triggers a batched load
if the profile is not yet cached.

Multiple cells calling `useProfile` in the same render frame get their
requests batched into a single `bulkResolve` (50ms window via
`createBatcher`).

functions.label: useProfileCache
description: Get the

instance (stable, never changes identity).

Returns `undefined` when user profiles are not configured.

functions.label: useProfileCacheVersion
description:
Subscribe to cache version changes.

Re-renders only when profiles are actually loaded (version bumps).
Use alongside `useProfileCache()` when you need reactive access to
the cache contents (e.g. field definitions that recompute on cache change).

functions.label: useQueryModel
description: Parse `queryText` into a

.

This is a memoized view — the model is recomputed only when `queryText`
or field/flag definitions change. It is never stored in state.

functions.label: useTagFilterToggle
description: Convenience wrapper around
for the `tag` field.

interfaces.label: ActiveFilters
description:
Active filters applied to the content list.

Filter dimensions (e.g. `tag`, `type`, `lastResponse`) are keyed by their
`fieldName` and hold an

. Boolean flag filters
(e.g. `starred`) use

. The index signature is
required to allow arbitrary filter dimensions, but note:

- The `search` key is always `string | undefined` — access it directly.
- Use

to narrow to `IncludeExcludeFilter`.
- Use
to narrow to `IncludeExcludeFlag`.

children.label: search
description: Search text extracted from the search bar, without filter syntax.

interfaces.label: ContentListFeatures
description: Feature configuration for enabling/customizing content list capabilities.

children.label: sorting
description: Sorting configuration.

children.label: pagination
description: Pagination configuration. Set to `false` to disable pagination entirely.

children.label: search
description: Search configuration.

children.label: selection
description:
Selection configuration.
When `true` (default), row selection checkboxes are shown and bulk
actions are enabled. Set to `false` to disable selection entirely.
Selection is automatically disabled when `isReadOnly` is `true`.

children.label: tags
description:
Tags feature configuration.

- `true` or `undefined`: Auto-enabled when `services.tags` is provided.
- `false`: Explicitly disables tags even if `services.tags` is present.
- `FilterFacetConfig`: Enables tags with popover facets from `getFacets`.

children.label: starred
description:
Starred feature configuration.

- `true` or `undefined`: Auto-enabled when `services.favorites` is provided.
- `false`: Explicitly disables starring even if `services.favorites` is present.

children.label: userProfiles
description:
User profiles feature configuration (createdBy filter, etc.).

- `true` or `undefined`: Auto-enabled when `services.userProfiles` is provided.
- `false`: Explicitly disables user profile features even if the service is present.
- `FilterFacetConfig`: Enables user profiles with popover facets from `getFacets`.

children.label: fields
description:
Additional field definitions for consumer-specific filter dimensions
(e.g. `updatedBy`, `type`, `status`).

Merged with built-in field definitions (tag, createdBy) in `useFieldDefinitions`.
Each entry registers a field name in the search bar schema and provides
ID ↔ display resolution for query parsing.

children.label: flags
description:
Additional flag definitions for consumer-specific boolean toggles
(e.g. `is:managed`, `is:deprecated`).

Merged with built-in flag definitions (starred) in `useFieldDefinitions`.

interfaces.label: ContentListItemConfig
description: Per-item configuration for link behavior and actions.

children.label: getHref
description:
Function to generate the href for an item link.
When provided, item titles become clickable links.

children.label: getEditUrl
description:
Function to generate the edit URL for an item.
When provided, the edit action renders as an `<a>` link with this `href`,
preserving native link behavior (right-click, middle-click, screen reader
announcement).

Composable with `onEdit`: when both are provided, the action renders as a
link (`href`) and also calls `onEdit` on click (e.g., for analytics tracking).

children.label: onEdit
description:
Callback invoked when the edit action is clicked.
Use this for side effects such as opening a flyout, tracking analytics,
or programmatic navigation.

When provided alone, the action renders as a button with an `onClick` handler.
When provided alongside `getEditUrl`, both are applied: the action renders
as a link and the callback fires on click.

children.label: onDelete
description:
Callback invoked to delete one or more items.
When provided, enables the delete action on rows and the bulk-delete flow.
The callback should handle the actual deletion and return a resolved promise on success.

children.label: onInspect
description:
Callback invoked to inspect an item (view/edit its metadata).
When provided, enables the "View details" row action.

This is a simple, UI-agnostic callback — the implementation decides what
happens when the action fires (e.g., opening a flyout, navigating to a
detail page). The Kibana content editor integration is handled by
`ContentListClientProvider` in `@kbn/content-list-provider-client`.

interfaces.label: ContentListLabels
description:
User-facing labels for the content type.

These should be i18n-translated strings for display in the UI.

children.label: entity
description: Singular form of the entity name (e.g., "dashboard"). Should be i18n-translated.

children.label: entityPlural
description: Plural form of the entity name (e.g., "dashboards"). Should be i18n-translated.

children.label: searchPlaceholder
description: Placeholder text for the search input. Should be i18n-translated.

interfaces.label: ContentListQueryData
description:
Query data returned from React Query.

This is read-only state derived from the data fetching layer.

children.label: items
description: Currently loaded items (transformed for rendering).

children.label: totalItems
description: Total number of items matching the current query (for pagination).

children.label: isLoading
description:
Whether the initial data load is in progress (no data available yet).

This is `true` only on the first fetch before any data has been received.
Use this for hard loading states (e.g., skeletons, spinners) when there is
nothing to show. Once data has been loaded, subsequent fetches triggered by
filter or search changes keep `isLoading` as `false` while the previous
data remains visible.

children.label: isFetching
description:
Whether a fetch is currently in progress (including background refetches).

Unlike `isLoading`, this is `true` during any fetch, including background
refetches triggered by search, sort, or filter changes. Use this for subtle
loading indicators (e.g., progress bar, reduced opacity) that should not
hide existing content.

children.label: error
description: Error from the most recent fetch attempt.

interfaces.label: ContentListQueryModel
description:
The query model — single source of truth for search, filters, and flags.

- `search`: free text (no field syntax).
- `filters`: field-based include/exclude filters, keyed by field name.
  Values are internal IDs (e.g., tag IDs, user UIDs). Display values
  (tag names, emails) are resolved via

.
- `flags`: boolean toggles (e.g., `starred`). Rendered as `is:flagName`
  in query text.

This object is JSON-serializable and UI-independent.

children.label: search
description: Free text search portion (field syntax already extracted).

children.label: filters
description: Field-based filters. Key = field name, value = include/exclude IDs.

children.label: flags
description: Boolean flags (e.g., `{ starred: true }`).

children.label: referencedFields
description:
Field names that had at least one clause with a non-empty value in the
parsed query AST, regardless of whether the value resolved to an ID.

Unlike `filters` (which only contains fields whose values resolved),
this set captures field-clause *presence*. Used to gate lazy operations
(e.g. profile priming) that must fire before resolution can succeed.

children.label: unresolvedFields
description:
Field names whose clause values fell through to the raw display-value
fallback in `resolveDisplayValue` — meaning `resolveDisplayToId` returned
`undefined` and no fuzzy match was found.

Only fields with lazy/async resolution (e.g. user-profile fields whose
backing store is populated asynchronously) should be treated as a
transient "resolving" state. Fields with synchronous resolution (e.g.
tags) appear here when the display value simply doesn't exist — that is
a valid empty result, not a loading condition.

interfaces.label: ContentListServices
description:
Services provided to the content list provider to enable additional capabilities.

Each service follows a pattern where providing the service enables the corresponding
feature by default. Features can be explicitly disabled via the `features` prop
even when the service is present.

children.label: tags
description:
Tags service for tag-based filtering and display.

Uses the standard `ContentManagementTagsServices` interface from `@kbn/content-management-tags`.
Provides `getTagList` for listing available tags and optionally `parseSearchQuery`
for extracting tag filters from the search bar query text.

children.label: favorites
description:
Favorites service for starring items.

Uses the `FavoritesClientPublic` interface from `@kbn/content-management-favorites-public`.
When provided, enables the starred feature (star button in table rows, starred
filter in toolbar) unless explicitly disabled via `features.starred: false`.

children.label: userProfiles
description:
User profiles service for user-based filtering.

When provided, enables the `createdBy` filter (and future `updatedBy`, `owner`,
etc.) unless explicitly disabled via `features.userProfiles: false`.

interfaces.label: ContentListSupports
description:
Resolved feature support flags.

These flags represent the **effective** availability of features after evaluating:
- Explicit configuration (e.g., `features.sorting: true` or `features.sorting: { ... }`)
- Implicit enablement (e.g., providing required services enabling a feature)
- Explicit disablement (e.g., `features.sorting: false` overrides defaults)
- Implicit disablement (e.g., missing services)

Use these flags to conditionally render UI elements or enable functionality based on
what's actually available, rather than checking raw configuration values.

children.label: sorting
description: Whether sorting is supported.

children.label: pagination
description: Whether pagination is supported.

children.label: search
description: Whether search is supported.

children.label: selection
description: Whether item selection and bulk actions are supported.

children.label: tags
description: Whether tags filtering and display is supported.

children.label: starred
description: Whether starring items is supported.

children.label: userProfiles
description: Whether user profile filtering (createdBy) is supported.

interfaces.label: ContentListUserProfilesServices
description:
User profiles service for user-based filtering (createdBy, updatedBy, etc.).

Provides async methods for fetching user profiles. The provider manages
a shared

so that all consumers (field definitions,
filter popovers, table cells) read synchronously from a single cache.

children.label: bulkResolve
description: Resolve specific UIDs to profiles (for on-demand loading).

interfaces.label: DataSourceConfig
description: Data source configuration properties.

children.label: findItems
description: Fetches items from the data source.

children.label: debounceMs
description:
Debounce delay in milliseconds applied to query-parameter changes before
a new `findItems` request is issued.

Defaults to

(300 ms), matching the Table List
View's built-in fetch debounce. Set to `0` to disable debouncing.

children.label: onInvalidate
description:
Called automatically before every explicit `refetch()` (e.g. after a
delete or create) so the next `findItems` call hits the server instead
of returning stale cached data.

Client-side providers that cache the server response between calls
should set this to clear that cache. Server-based providers that
always call through can omit it.

children.label: onRefresh
description:
Lightweight refresh: re-decorates cached items with external data
(e.g. starred status) without a full server refetch.

children.label: onFetchSuccess
description: Called after successful fetch.

interfaces.label: DeleteConfirmationComponentProps
description: Props for the presentational

children.label: items
description: Items being deleted.

children.label: entityName
description: Singular entity name (e.g., "dashboard").

children.label: entityNamePlural
description: Plural entity name (e.g., "dashboards").

children.label: isDeleting
description: Whether a delete operation is currently executing.

children.label: error
description: Error message from the last failed attempt, displayed inline.

children.label: onCancel
description: Called when the user cancels.

children.label: onConfirm
description: Called when the user confirms.

interfaces.label: DeleteConfirmationModalProps
description: Props for the connected

children.label: items
description: Items to delete.

children.label: onClose
description: Called when the modal should close (cancel or successful delete).

interfaces.label: FieldDefinition
description:
Defines how a field-based filter maps between internal IDs and display values.

Used by the parser (display→ID) and serializer (ID→display) to convert
between query text and the query model. Adding a new filter dimension
(e.g., `updatedBy`, `owner`) only requires providing a new `FieldDefinition`.

children.label: fieldName
description: Field name used in query syntax (e.g., `'tag'`, `'createdBy'`).

children.label: resolveIdToDisplay
description: Resolve an internal ID to the display value shown in query text.

children.label: resolveDisplayToId
description: Resolve a display value (from query text) to an internal ID. Returns `undefined` if unresolvable.

children.label: resolveFuzzyDisplayToIds
description:
Fuzzy/prefix match: resolve a partial display value to all matching IDs.

Called when `resolveDisplayToId` returns `undefined` (no exact match).
Returns all IDs whose display value contains the input (case-insensitive).
Used for incremental search: typing `createdBy:j` matches "jane" and "john".

Optional — when absent, unresolvable values are silently dropped.

interfaces.label: FilterFacet
description:
A single display-ready facet for a filter popover option.

The `T` parameter carries provider-specific data through the facet pipeline,
e.g. `FilterFacet<Tag>` for tag facets or `FilterFacet<UserProfileEntry>`
for user profile facets. Renderers receive the typed `data` without casting.

children.label: key
description: Unique key identifying this facet (e.g. user UID, tag ID).

children.label: label
description: Human-readable display label.

children.label: count
description: Optional item count for this facet value.

children.label: data
description: Optional provider-specific data (e.g. `UserProfileEntry` for profiles, `Tag` for tags).

interfaces.label: FilterFacetConfig
description:
Provider that supplies display-ready facets for a filter popover.

The `T` parameter flows through to each

returned by
`getFacets`, keeping the `data` payload typed end-to-end.

How the implementation accesses the item set is provider-specific:
- Client provider: captures `getItems()` from the strategy via closure.
- Server provider: calls a server aggregation endpoint, ignoring items.

children.label: getFacets
description:
Fetch display-ready facets for the filter popover.
Called lazily when the popover opens, with its own React Query lifecycle.

interfaces.label: FilterFacetParams
description: Parameters passed to

children.label: filters
description: Current active filters (excluding the filter being fetched, for faceted-search semantics).

children.label: signal
description: Abort signal for request cancellation.

interfaces.label: FindItemsParams
description: Parameters for the `findItems` function.

children.label: searchQuery
description:
Search query text with filter syntax already extracted.

This is a convenience alias for `filters.search ?? ''`. When building
queries, prefer this over accessing `filters.search` directly.

children.label: filters
description:
Active filters (includes the raw `search` text and any structured filters).

The `createdBy` dimension may contain sentinel keys (
) in addition to real user UIDs. Use

to map items to matching keys when implementing
client-side filtering.

children.label: sort
description:
Sort configuration.

When sorting is disabled via `features.sorting: false`, this will be `undefined`.
Implementations should return items in their natural order (e.g., server default).

children.label: page
description: Pagination configuration.

children.label: signal
description: AbortSignal for request cancellation.

interfaces.label: FindItemsResult
description: Result from the `findItems` function.

children.label: items
description: Items for the current page.

children.label: total
description: Total matching items for pagination.

interfaces.label: FlagDefinition
description: Defines a boolean flag that appears as `is:flagName` in query text.

children.label: flagName
description: Flag name in query syntax (e.g., `'starred'` for `is:starred`).

children.label: modelKey
description: Key in `ContentListQueryModel.flags`.

interfaces.label: IncludeExcludeFilter
description: Include/exclude filter shape used by tag, type, lastResponse, and other filter dimensions.

children.label: include
description: Values that items must match (include filter).

children.label: exclude
description: Values that items must not match (exclude filter).

interfaces.label: PaginationConfig
description: Pagination configuration.

children.label: initialPageSize
description: Initial number of items per page. Defaults to

children.label: pageSizeOptions
description: Available page size options. Defaults to

interfaces.label: QueryFilterValue
description:
Include/exclude filter for a single field dimension.

Always uses arrays (never undefined) to avoid null-checking throughout.

children.label: include
description: IDs that items must match.

children.label: exclude
description: IDs that items must not match.

interfaces.label: SearchConfig
description:
Search configuration.

Controls the search input behavior in the toolbar.

children.label: initialSearch
description: Initial search text to populate on mount.

interfaces.label: SortField
description:
Simplified sort field definition.

The Sort component auto-generates asc/desc options from this definition.

children.label: field
description: Field to sort by (must match data field name).

children.label: name
description: Display name for the field (used to generate default labels).

children.label: ascLabel
description: Custom label for ascending sort (overrides auto-generated label).

children.label: descLabel
description: Custom label for descending sort (overrides auto-generated label).

interfaces.label: SortingConfig
description:
Sorting configuration.

Use `fields` for the simpler API where asc/desc options are auto-generated.
Use `options` for full control over each dropdown option.

children.label: fields
description: Simplified sortable fields - auto-generates asc/desc options for each field.

children.label: options
description:
Explicit sort options with full control over labels.
Ignored if `fields` is provided.

children.label: initialSort
description: Initial sort state.

interfaces.label: SortOption
description: Sort option definition with explicit label, field, and direction.

children.label: label
description: Display label for the sort option.

children.label: field
description: Field to sort by.

children.label: direction
description: Sort direction.

interfaces.label: UseContentListFiltersReturn
description: Return type for the
hook.

children.label: filters
description: Currently active filters (derived from query text and state).

children.label: clearFilters
description: Clear all filter and flag clauses from `queryText`, preserving free-text search.

interfaces.label: UseContentListPaginationReturn
description: Return type for the
hook.

children.label: isSupported
description: Whether pagination is supported (enabled via features).

children.label: pageIndex
description: Current page index (0-based).

children.label: pageSize
description: Current number of items per page.

children.label: totalItems
description: Total number of items matching the current query.

children.label: pageCount
description: Total number of pages.

children.label: pageSizeOptions
description: Available page size options for the dropdown.

children.label: setPageIndex
description: Navigate to a specific page index. No-op if pagination is disabled.

children.label: setPageSize
description: Change the page size (resets to page 0). No-op if pagination is disabled. Persists to `localStorage`.

interfaces.label: UseContentListSearchReturn
description: Return type for the
hook.

children.label: queryText
description: The query text — source of truth for the search bar.

children.label: setQueryFromEuiQuery
description:
Update query from an already-parsed EUI Query object (search bar typing).
Stores `query.text` as the new queryText.

children.label: setQueryFromText
description: Update query from raw text (programmatic input, URL params).

children.label: isSupported
description: Whether search is supported (enabled via features).

children.label: fieldNames
description:
Registered field names from field definitions.
Pass to `EuiSearchBar`'s `box.schema` so it recognizes filter fields.

interfaces.label: UseContentListSelectionReturn
description: Return type for the
hook.

children.label: selectedIds
description: IDs of currently selected items.

children.label: selectedItems
description: Currently selected items resolved from the loaded items list.

children.label: selectedCount
description: Number of currently selected items.

children.label: isSelected
description: Whether a specific item is selected.

children.label: setSelection
description:
Replace the selection with the given items.
Typically called by `EuiBasicTable`'s `onSelectionChange` callback.

children.label: clearSelection
description: Clear all selected items.

children.label: isSupported
description: Whether selection is supported (enabled via features, not read-only).

interfaces.label: UseContentListSortReturn
description: Return type for the `useContentListSort` hook.

children.label: field
description: Current sort field name.

children.label: direction
description: Current sort direction.

children.label: setSort
description: Updates the sort configuration. No-op if sorting is disabled.

children.label: isSupported
description: Whether sorting is supported (enabled via features).

interfaces.label: UseDeleteConfirmationOptions
description: Options for

children.label: onClose
description:
Called after the modal closes (cancel or successful delete).
Use for side effects like clearing selection state.

interfaces.label: UseDeleteConfirmationReturn
description: Return value of

children.label: requestDelete
description: Trigger the delete confirmation modal for the given items.

children.label: deleteModal
description: The modal element to render, or `null` when inactive.

interfaces.label: UserProfileEntry
description:
A user profile entry for filtering and display.

Carries the full
and optional

so that consumers (e.g. table cells) can use `UserAvatarTip` from
`@kbn/user-profile-components` without issuing their own per-row fetches.

The `email` and `fullName` convenience fields exist for query-bar resolution
(display↔ID mapping in
) and are derived from `user`.

children.label: uid
description: Internal user ID.

children.label: user
description: Full Kibana user info (username, email, full_name).

children.label: avatar
description: Optional avatar data (image URL, custom initials/color).

children.label: email
description:
Convenience alias for `user.email ?? ''`.
Used by query-bar display↔ID resolution.

children.label: fullName
description:
Convenience alias for `user.full_name ?? user.username`.
Used by query-bar display↔ID resolution.

misc.label: ContentListConfig
description: Complete configuration for a content list.

misc.label: ContentListCoreConfig
description: Core configuration - entity metadata and base settings.

misc.label: ContentListIdentity
description:
Identity configuration for content lists.

At least one of `id` or `queryKeyScope` must be provided:

- **`id` only**: `queryKeyScope` is derived as `${id}-listing`. Use when you want a simple
  identifier that also serves as the cache key base.

- **`queryKeyScope` only**: Use when you only need cache isolation without a semantic identifier.

- **Both**: `queryKeyScope` is used for caching/persistence, `id` is available for other
  purposes (e.g., `data-test-subj`, analytics).
signature: { id: string; queryKeyScope?: string | undefined; } | { id?: string | undefined; queryKeyScope: string; }

misc.label: ContentListItem
description:
Standardized item structure for rendering components (tables, grids, etc.).

This is the common interface that all rendering components work with,
regardless of the underlying datasource type. The `findItems` function
in `DataSourceConfig` must return items in this format.
signature: T & { id: string; title: string; description?: string | undefined; type?: string | undefined; updatedAt?: Date | undefined; tags?: string[] | undefined; createdAt?: string | undefined; createdBy?: string | undefined; updatedBy?: string | undefined; managed?: boolean | undefined; }

misc.label: ContentListProviderProps
description: Props for the `ContentListProvider` component.

misc.label: CREATED_BY_FILTER_ID
description: Filter dimension key for created-by user filtering.

misc.label: DEFAULT_SORT_FIELDS
description:
Default sort fields used when sorting is enabled but no explicit fields are configured.

Provides Name (A-Z / Z-A) and Last updated (Newest / Oldest) sort options,
matching the common pattern across `TableListView` consumers.

misc.label: FindItemsFn
description: Function signature for fetching items from a data source.

misc.label: IncludeExcludeFlag
description: Discriminated union for boolean flag filters (e.g. `starred`).

misc.label: MANAGED_USER_FILTER
description:
Sentinel filter key for managed items (e.g. bundled with a package).

Used in `getCreatorKey`, filter facets, and field definitions to
distinguish managed items from real user-authored ones.

misc.label: MANAGED_USER_LABEL
description: Display label for the
sentinel.

misc.label: NO_CREATOR_USER_FILTER
description:
Sentinel filter key for items with no `createdBy` value
(e.g. created via API or before Kibana 7.14).

misc.label: NO_CREATOR_USER_LABEL
description: Display label for the
sentinel.

misc.label: TAG_FILTER_ID
description: Filter dimension key for tag-based filtering. Matches the `fieldName` used in tag filter popovers.

objects.label: CONTENT_LIST_ACTIONS
description: Action type constants for state reducer.
signature: { readonly SET_QUERY: "SET_QUERY"; readonly RESET_QUERY: "RESET_QUERY"; readonly SET_SORT: "SET_SORT"; readonly SET_PAGE_INDEX: "SET_PAGE_INDEX"; readonly SET_PAGE_SIZE: "SET_PAGE_SIZE"; readonly SET_SELECTION: "SET_SELECTION"; readonly CLEAR_SELECTION: "CLEAR_SELECTION"; }

objects.label: contentListKeys
description:
Query keys for content list items.

Uses `queryKeyScope` (a stable identifier) to prevent cache collisions.
Intentionally does not use user-facing labels (which may be i18n-translated)
to ensure cache stability across locales.

children.label: all
description:
/**
   * Base query key for all content list queries.
   *
   * @param queryKeyScope - Stable scope identifier for cache isolation.
   */

children.label: items
description:
/**
   * Query key for items queries with specific parameters.
   *
   * @param queryKeyScope - Stable scope identifier for cache isolation.
   * @param params - Query parameters (search, filters, sort, page).
   */

children.label: profiles
description:
/**
   * Query key for the shared user profile cache.
   *
   * @param queryKeyScope - Stable scope identifier for cache isolation.
   */

children.label: filterFacets
description:
/**
   * Query key for filter facets (popover options with counts).
   *
   * @param queryKeyScope - Stable scope identifier for cache isolation.
   * @param filterId - Filter dimension key (e.g. `'createdBy'`, `'tag'`).
   * @param filters - Active filters excluding the filter being fetched.
   */

objects.label: contentListQueryClient
description:
Shared React Query client for content list queries.

Uses conservative defaults:
- No automatic retries (errors surface immediately).
- Standard stale time and cache time.

objects.label: DEFAULT_INITIAL_SORT
description:
Default initial sort used when sorting is enabled but no explicit `initialSort` is configured.

Sorts by `title` ascending (A-Z), matching `TableListView` behavior.
Consumers that prefer "newest first" can set `initialSort: { field: 'updatedAt', direction: 'desc' }`.

children.label: field
description:
Default initial sort used when sorting is enabled but no explicit `initialSort` is configured.

Sorts by `title` ascending (A-Z), matching `TableListView` behavior.
Consumers that prefer "newest first" can set `initialSort: { field: 'updatedAt', direction: 'desc' }`.

children.label: direction
description:
Default initial sort used when sorting is enabled but no explicit `initialSort` is configured.

Sorts by `title` ascending (A-Z), matching `TableListView` behavior.
Consumers that prefer "newest first" can set `initialSort: { field: 'updatedAt', direction: 'desc' }`.

objects.label: EMPTY_MODEL
description: Empty query model.

children.label: search
description: Empty query model.

children.label: filters
description: Empty query model.

children.label: flags
description: Empty query model.

children.label: referencedFields
description: Empty query model.

children.label: unresolvedFields
description: Empty query model.

description: React context for the shared

.

`undefined` when user profiles are not configured.

objects.label: SENTINEL_KEYS
description: The set of sentinel filter keys (not real user UIDs).
